summary: Retest of DeviceEditModal.save() silence-fix. PRIMARY FRONTEND FIX VERIFIED: clicking 'Salva' now correctly fires PUT /api/connector/{cid}/managed-devices/{did}/silence with body {silenced:true, reason:'Test silence E2E'} (previously in iteration_63 this PUT never fired). Also verified: the 3 try/catch blocks are independent — the error toast after save lists all three failures ('Metodo monitoraggio', 'SNMP', 'Silenzio alert') proving silence is attempted even after monitor-type/snmp fail (resilience confirmed). HOWEVER end-to-end persistence could NOT be validated because backend returns 404 'Device non trovato' for device 192.168.1.3 on all 3 PUT endpoints. Root cause is a data/architectural mismatch (not a regression): ClientOverviewPage reads devices from GET /api/devices?client_id=... (returns id=alert-hub-dev-1 for 192.168.1.3), but the silence/monitor-type/snmp PUT endpoints look up the device in the managed_devices collection which is empty for this client (GET /api/connector/da3d6e40.../managed-devices → 0 items). Consequently the silence-badge, reopen-state, silence-OFF, and resilience-persistence sub-tests cannot be green-lit end-to-end until backend device_id lookup is unified.

critical.issue: Returns 404 'Device non trovato' for device_id=alert-hub-dev-1 (IP 192.168.1.3) even though the device is listed by GET /api/devices?client_id=.... The managed_devices collection used by these PUT endpoints is empty for client da3d6e40-b3e5-4d46-9787-dde328a3aa36. Either /api/devices should hydrate managed_devices, or the PUT handlers should fall back to the /api/devices source. Note: review request referenced device_id=alert-hub-dev-1 which does NOT exist in either collection — seed may have drifted since iteration_63.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend correctly dispatches PUT /silence with proper payload and handles 3 independent try/catches, but because backend 404s, silencePersisted stays false, no success toast ('Alert SILENZIATI…') fires, and silence-badge-192.168.1.3 never renders in the table. The UI error path works as designed (toast lists all 3 errors).

action_items: Main agent: fix backend device lookup mismatch — PUT /silence/monitor-type/snmp handlers on /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices/{device_id}/... must resolve device_id against the same source as GET /api/devices?client_id=..., OR ensure managed_devices is hydrated/synced when a device appears in /api/devices.

Main agent: verify current seed data for client da3d6e40-b3e5-4d46-9787-dde328a3aa36 — review-request referenced device_id=alert-hub-dev-1 but current /api/devices returns id=alert-hub-dev-1 for 192.168.1.3 (likely reseed).
After backend fix: retest the full flow (silence-ON → badge → reopen pre-filled → silence-OFF → resilience with invalid monitor-type).
critical_code_review_comments: DeviceEditModal.js save() is now well-structured: 3 independent try/catch blocks, explicit silencePersisted flag, dirty-state check (alertsSilenced !== wasSilenced || silenceReason !== wasReason), and specific success toast for the silence branch. Good fix.

The modal intentionally does NOT close on save (per design — user may click 'Applica ora' next). UX is acceptable but consider a hint below Salva such as 'Le modifiche sono salvate. Chiudi o clicca Applica ora per forzare il polling.' to reduce confusion.

Backend architectural issue: two parallel device sources (/api/devices vs /api/connector/.../managed-devices) without a single source of truth is a latent bug generator. Recommend unification or at minimum a server-side mapping layer.

success_rate.backend: not retested (prior 100% per iteration_63) but current /silence endpoint effectively returns 404 for target device due to data mismatch
success_rate.frontend: 100% of the specific Salva-wiring fix verified (PUT fires, correct payload, 3 independent try/catches, error toast lists all 3); 0% of end-to-end persistence flow (badge/reopen/off/resilience) verifiable due to backend 404

seed_data_creation: No seed created. Current client da3d6e40-b3e5-4d46-9787-dde328a3aa36 has device 192.168.1.3 listed in /api/devices (id=alert-hub-dev-1, name='Auto-192.168.1.3', device_type=switch) but NOT in /api/connector/.../managed-devices (empty). No cleanup needed (silence never persisted).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The Salva-button wiring fix is definitively verified — do NOT retest that narrow piece. The blocker is a backend data-source mismatch between /api/devices (used by UI table + modal) and managed_devices collection (used by PUT /silence|/monitor-type|/snmp endpoints). Once the main agent unifies these or hydrates managed_devices from /api/devices, re-run the full flow: (1) toggle ON + reason → Salva → expect 200 + 'Alert SILENZIATI' toast → close modal → verify [data-testid='silence-badge-192.168.1.3'] with text 'ALERT OFF'; (2) reopen modal → toggle already checked + reason prefilled; (3) uncheck → Salva → 200 + 'Alert RIATTIVATI' toast → badge gone; (4) GET /api/connector/{cid}/managed-devices to confirm alerts_silenced persists. Also check review-request device_id (3f20edc0-...) vs current actual id (1089e659-...) — may need to align docs or reseed.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Frontend fix is correct and complete. The backend has two device sources: /api/devices?client_id=... (what the UI reads) and /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices (what the PUT silence/monitor-type/snmp endpoints validate against). They are NOT synchronized for auto-discovered devices, so a device visible in the UI may not exist for the PUT endpoints → 404.
rca_of_the_issue.evidence: Playwright observed PUT https://snmp-hub-noc.preview.emergentagent.com/api/connector/da3d6e40.../managed-devices/1089e659-b593-42bf-bb9c-a18aa990e6a9/silence with correct body {silenced:true,reason:'Test silence E2E'} returned HTTP 404 body {detail:'Device non trovato'}. Direct curl to /api/connector/da3d6e40.../managed-devices (auth as admin) returned 0 devices. The same 404 occurred for the /monitor-type and /snmp PUT calls, proving all 3 try/catches are independently invoked (resilience proven).
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Backend: either (a) insert/sync auto-discovered devices into managed_devices collection, or (b) make the PUT handlers lookup device_id across both sources. Frontend fix itself requires no changes.
